paper_map.title: Microstructural Evolution and Mechanical Properties of Nb-Containing Medium-Mn Low-Density Steels
paper_map.abstract_summary: This study investigates the effect of Nb microalloying (0.02 and 0.04 wt%) on the microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe-12Mn-9Al-3Cr-1.4C low-density steels. Nb addition refines austenite grains by NbC precipitation and increases the formation energy of κ-carbide, impeding its coarsening. The yield strength of 0.04Nb steel is ≈71 MPa higher than 0.02Nb steel, primarily due to enhanced fine-grain and dislocation strengthening contributions. Quantitative analysis of strengthening mechanisms provides insights for alloy design.
paper_map.research_objective: To elucidate the role of Nb in regulating nano-scale precipitation (NbC and κ-carbide) and corresponding strengthening mechanisms in medium-Mn low-density steel.
